items.title: [正面] A jank, AI translated, fever dream of a game, it f...
items.summary:
A jank, AI translated, fever dream of a game, it feels like it is held together with bubblegum and duct tape. Yet they must have done something right as i am immensely enjoying it.

The AI weirdness and story jank are oddly appropriate, it feels like a HP Lovecraft book, not reading one but like being the protagonist who is already half way insane.

The gacha feels generous and the gameplay is enjoyable, like slay the spire so a bit of thought is required.

items.title: [正面] Although this game is almost one year old, it is n...
items.summary:
Although this game is almost one year old, it is not popular at all, and I can't see it become it in the future. Yet, the problem is not that it is a bad game, but that is everything but a casual-friendly lightweight game. Morimens is not the kind of game that you can pick and put down quickly; it has long battle stages, which also become quite hard as the game progresses and require strategy and to read a lot. An additional reason which might keep some players away are the lovecraftian-styled story, arts and characters, and how the story is pretty dark overall. Although it is true that The Limbus Company exists and is capable of thriving.

Morimens consists of a Slay the Spire-like combat system (so it's a deck builder) with tons of original additions and mechanics. The gameplay is very good and well-crafted, and also somewhat original. The designs are also very good and the game has a cool artstyle.

Its main weaknesses are the fact that the English translation is still in beta, the gameplay rhythm which is pretty heavy, and the fact that the majority of characters is limited and not present in the standard banners.
